data.title: Senator John Boozman postpones crypto bill markup to January end for broad support, cites need for bipartisan consensus.
data.description: The Senate Agriculture Committee, led by Chairman John Boozman (R-AR), has decided to delay the markup of the cryptocurrency market structure bill from Thursday to the final week of January. The postponement is aimed at allowing more time for bipartisan consensus to be reached. Boozman mentioned that the committee had productive bipartisan discussions over the weekend and had made significant progress. He emphasized the importance of finalizing the remaining details to ensure broad support for the legislation. The decision to postpone the markup was made to refine the bill further before proceeding.

data.title: MangoBoost debuts 400G DPU tech at major events in 2025, highlighting AI infrastructure solutions.
data.description: In Bellevue, Washington, MangoBoost unveiled its cutting-edge 400G data acceleration technologies in 2025. The company presented live demonstrations at key industry events, highlighting the application of full-stack networking acceleration to address AI infrastructure issues. At the OCP Global Summit and SC25 conferences, MangoBoost showcased its 400G BoostX™ DPU, GPUBoost™ RDMA NIC, and LLMBoost AI Enterprise software. These technologies aim to tackle real-world challenges in the AI sector, offering innovative solutions for enhanced performance.

data.title: Trump urges tech firms to cover power costs amid surging US demand, with Microsoft leading by making changes this week.
data.description: The President of the United States has called on technology giants to bear the expenses of powering data centers as the country's energy consumption sharply increased. Microsoft has been the pioneer in implementing these changes, with others expected to follow suit.

data.title: WuXi Biologics in China receives GMP certification from UK MHRA for ophthalmic biologic manufacturing. 2269.HK stock symbol.
data.description: In Wuxi, China on January 12, 2026, WuXi Biologics, a prominent Contract Research, Development, and Manufacturing Organization (CRDMO) traded as 2269.HK, revealed updates on its facilities. It specifically mentioned advancements in two manufacturing sites in Wuxi, namely Drug Product Facility 5 (DP5) and Drug Product Packaging Center (DPPC).

data.title: Kevin Hassett, Director of National Economic Council, praises Trump's policies for booming economy despite high interest rates.
data.description: Kevin Hassett, Director of the National Economic Council, recently defended the performance of the U.S. economy during President Donald Trump's tenure, citing its strength despite the Federal Reserve's tight monetary policy. Hassett declared on CNBC's "Squawk Box" that the economy is thriving due to Trump's policies rather than the actions of the Federal Reserve. He expressed Trump's frustration with the Fed's high interest rates, emphasizing that this discontent was unrelated to the Department of Justice's consideration of a "criminal indictment" against Fed Chair Jerome Powell. Hassett dismissed the idea of giving the Fed credit for avoiding obstructing positive economic developments and called for more self-reflection from central bankers, highlighting the need to prevent inflation from spiraling out of control.

data.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer's aides placed on leave amid misconduct investigation by Labor Department following complaint.
data.description: An inquiry by the Labor Department is underway following a complaint accusing Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer of misconduct.

data.title: Meta plans to reduce 10% of its metaverse arm this week, a move linked to their AI focus as metaverse spending drops.
data.description: Reality Labs at Meta is facing reduction as the company's budget for the metaverse declines. This shift comes as Meta focuses more on the development of artificial intelligence technologies.

data.title: Nam Tai Property announces 6-year master lease with Shenzhen Anju, de-risking project with state-owned partner.
data.description: Nam Tai Property Inc. has reached a major milestone in its Technology Center project in Shenzhen, China. The subsidiary, Nam Tai Investment, has secured a six-year master lease agreement with Shenzhen Anju Leyu Development & Construction Co. Ltd. The state-owned partner, Shenzhen Anju, is highly reputable. This partnership reduces risks and enhances the project's stability. The lease agreement spans six years, highlighting the long-term commitment of both parties. The Company, listed on the OTC Expert Market under ticker symbol NTPIF, expressed satisfaction with this development.

data.title: US Senate Agriculture Committee postpones crypto bill markup, aiming for bipartisan backing; now set for end of month.
data.description: The Chairman of the Senate Agriculture Committee, John Boozman, has announced that the work on a bill related to the structure of the cryptocurrency market has been postponed. This delay is intended to allow more time to gather bipartisan support for the legislation. The bill, which aims to regulate the crypto market, will be revisited once additional support from both sides of the aisle is secured. This decision highlights the importance of bipartisan cooperation in developing policies related to the cryptocurrency industry.
